news.title: Moms, Coaches, Doctors, Entrepreneurs: Who Are America’s Health and Wellness Influencers?
news.summary_en: Half of U.S. adults under 50 say they get health and wellness information from social media influencers or podcasts. About 4 in 10 of these influencers describe themselves as health care professionals; coaches and entrepreneurs are almost as common.

news.title: 71% of U.S. homeowners say their home insurance costs have gone up
news.summary_en: The two reasons homeowners cite most often are insurance companies wanting to make more money and the costs of repairing and rebuilding.

news.title: Many Americans hold utility companies responsible for their rising home energy bills
news.summary_en: Three-quarters of U.S. adults say their home energy costs have gone up in recent years, including 42% who say these costs have gone up a lot.

news.title: How question wording affects polling on the morality of homosexuality
news.summary_en: Americans largely don’t distinguish between the morality of “homosexuality” and “homosexual behavior,” though some subgroups may, according to a new survey experiment.

news.title: About half of Americans continue to say Trump administration is doing ‘too much’ on deportations
news.summary_en: The share saying the administration is doing too little to deport immigrants living in the country illegally has risen slightly, mainly driven by Republicans.
